texto: Yet the Narrator’s quest is not only for his own identity and vocation. He seeks an understanding of art, sexuality and worldly and political affairs: he is a snoop and a voyeur; he comments and classifies; his taxonomic impulse makes the novel appear to be a vast compendium, replete with burrowing wasps and bedsteads, military strategies, stereoscopes, asparagus and aeroplanes.
explicacao: Citação de Adam A. Watt sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “Yet the Narrator’s quest is not only for his own identity and vocation. He seeks an understanding…” articula sabedoria, curiosity, gnothi-seauton em registro de sabedoria, com relevância para contextos como termino e gnothi-seauton.
